title: Phase 1 spec: tools — MCP (MCPTool, McpAuth, List/ReadMcpResource + services/mcp) [CC parity]
description:
## Goal

Extract a behavioral spec for this subsystem by reading Claude Code's TypeScript source. **Output**: update this issue body with a markdown spec pinning down each testable contract (input → expected output, edge cases, error paths). No Rust yet — that is Phase 2.

Parent audit: #523.

## Claude Code source — read these

- `services/mcp/client.ts` (~3348 lines)
- `services/mcp/config.ts` (~1578 lines)
- `services/mcp/useManageMCPConnections.ts` (~1141 lines)
- `services/mcp/elicitationHandler.ts` (~313 lines) — server-prompts-user mid-call
- `services/mcp/xaa.ts` (~511 lines) — enterprise IdP OAuth
- `services/mcp/xaaIdpLogin.ts` (~487 lines)
- `services/mcp/channelPermissions.ts` (~240 lines) — per-server permissions
- `tools/McpAuthTool/McpAuthTool.ts` (~215 lines)

## OpenClaudia counterpart

- `src/mcp.rs` (~1084 lines) — `McpServer::new` 360-438, `refresh_tools` 439-476, capability check 418

---

## Behavioral spec

### B1 — Handshake: `McpServer::new` / `connectToServer`

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:595-1641` — `connectToServer` (memoized). Transport selection is exhaustive: `stdio` (default), `sse`, `sse-ide`, `ws`, `ws-ide`, `http` (StreamableHTTP), `sdk` (in-process via `SdkControlClientTransport`), `claudeai-proxy`. For `stdio` the SDK `StdioClientTransport` spawns the subprocess; for `http` a `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` is used. After transport construction, `client.connect(transport)` races a connection timeout (`getConnectionTimeoutMs()`, env-controlled). The connected client declares capabilities `{roots:{}, elicitation:{}}`. A `ListRootsRequest` handler is registered immediately (returns `file://<cwd>`). Handshake result is memoized via `getServerCacheKey` so re-calls return the cached connection; `onclose` clears the cache to allow reconnection.

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:360-432` — `McpServer::new` calls `self.initialize()` then `self.refresh_tools()`. `initialize()` sends `initialize` with `protocolVersion: "2024-11-05"` and `clientInfo: {name:"openclaudia", version:<pkg>}`, declares only `capabilities.roots.listChanged:true` (no `elicitation` capability). Then sends `notifications/initialized` (fire-and-forget, `.ok()` discards error). No connection-establishment timeout. Memoization absent — each call to `McpManager::connect_stdio` / `connect_http` creates a fresh server.

**Input**: `(name: &str, transport: Box<dyn McpTransport>)` — transport already spawned/connected by caller.

**Output**: `Result<McpServer, McpError>` containing populated `capabilities` and `tools` on success.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: If `stdio` subprocess's `stderr` emits before connect, it is captured and logged after connect.
- CC: Handshake timeout fires after `getConnectionTimeoutMs()` ms, calls `transport.close()` and rejects.
- OC: `notifications/initialized` failure is silently swallowed (`.ok()`). Missing in CC which uses SDK (SDK sends notification internally).
- OC: `protocolVersion` pinned to `"2024-11-05"` (2024 spec); CC delegates version negotiation to the MCP SDK library.

**Error path**:
- CC: `connectToServer` catches and returns `{type:'failed', error:string}` — no exception escapes. Auth failures (401/`UnauthorizedError`) return `{type:'needs-auth'}`.
- OC: `McpServer::new` propagates `McpError` to caller; no failed-vs-connected distinction — caller must handle `Err`.

**Gap**: OC does not declare `elicitation` capability in the `initialize` params → server will not send elicitation requests to OC even if it would to CC. Filed as gap issue (see below).

---

### B2 — Tool discovery: `refresh_tools` / `fetchToolsForClient`

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:1743-1998` — `fetchToolsForClient` (LRU-memoized, size 20, keyed by server name). Guards on `client.capabilities?.tools` being truthy; returns `[]` immediately if absent. Issues `tools/list` via SDK `client.request(…, ListToolsResultSchema)`. Results are `recursivelySanitizeUnicode`-cleaned. Each tool is wrapped in a `Tool` object with: qualified name `mcp__<server>__<tool>` (or bare name in `CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_MCP_NO_PREFIX` mode), `isMcp:true`, `isConcurrencySafe`/`isReadOnly` from `tool.annotations.readOnlyHint`, `isDestructive` from `destructiveHint`, `isOpenWorld` from `openWorldHint`, description truncated to 2048 chars. `supports_tool_list_changed` is derived from `capabilities.tools.listChanged`.

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:439-476` — `refresh_tools` issues `tools/list` unconditionally (no capability guard). Populates `self.tools: Vec<McpTool>`. Logs `list_changed_supported` from `capabilities.tools.list_changed`. `supports_tool_list_changed()` at line 468-473 reads `capabilities.tools.list_changed`. No LRU memoization; no Unicode sanitization; no annotation fields (readOnly, destructive, openWorld) extracted; no description truncation. Tool qualified name follows `mcp__<server>__<tool>` convention (line 667 of `McpManager::tools_as_openai_functions`).

**Input**: No explicit inputs — reads server-cached capability state. CC adds `{_meta: {claudecode/toolUseId}}` passthrough on call.

**Output**: Mutates `self.tools` in OC; returns `Vec<Tool>` in CC.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: `tools/list` failure logs and returns `[]`, never propagates error to caller.
- OC: `tools/list` failure propagates `McpError` to caller of `refresh_tools`.
- CC: SDK type may return `undefined` tools array; OC: `result.get("tools").and_then(|t| t.as_array())` also handles absence gracefully.
- OC: `refresh_tools` is called even when `capabilities.tools` is `None` (no guard at line 440). CC checks `client.capabilities?.tools` first.

**Error path**:
- CC: `fetchToolsForClient` wraps entire body in `try/catch`, logs error, returns `[]`.
- OC: Returns `McpError` propagated from `self.transport.request(…)`.

**Discovered gap**: OC calls `tools/list` unconditionally, ignoring the `capabilities.tools` gate. CC skips the call when `capabilities.tools` is falsy. Filed as gap.

---

### B3 — Tool call: `call_tool` / `callMCPTool`

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:3029-3245` — `callMCPTool`. Issues `client.callTool({name, arguments, _meta}, CallToolResultSchema, {signal, timeout, onprogress})`. Runs its own `Promise.race` against a custom `timeoutMs` timer (default ~27.8 hours, overridden by `MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS` env var). Sets a 30-second interval logging "still running". On success, checks `result.isError`: if true, extracts text from `result.content[0].text` or `result.error` (legacy) and throws `McpToolCallError`. On success, calls `processMCPResult` which handles `toolResult` (legacy string), `structuredContent` (serialized JSON + schema), or `content[]` array (text/image/blob items). Images are resized/compressed via `maybeResizeAndDownsampleImageBuffer`. Large content may be persisted to disk and replaced with a file path reference. Auth errors (401/`UnauthorizedError`) throw `McpAuthError`. Session expiry (404/-32001 or -32000 on HTTP) throws `McpSessionExpiredError` and clears connection cache. `AbortError` (user Esc) returns `{content: undefined}` silently.

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:487-502` — `McpServer::call_tool`. Pre-checks `self.tools.iter().any(|t| t.name == name)`; returns `McpError::ToolNotFound` if absent. Issues `tools/call` with `{name, arguments}`. Returns raw `Value` (the full JSON-RPC result). No `isError` flag inspection. No image handling. No large-output persistence. No session-expiry detection. No progress callbacks. No per-call `_meta` passthrough. Timeout via `McpManager::call_tool_with_timeout` wraps with `tokio::time::timeout` returning `McpError::Timeout`.

**Input**:
- CC: `{client, tool:string, args:Record<string,unknown>, meta?, signal:AbortSignal, onProgress?}`.
- OC: `(name: &str, arguments: Value)` — no signal, no meta, no progress.

**Output**:
- CC: `{content: MCPToolResult, _meta?, structuredContent?}` — normalized, image-compressed, possibly file-persisted.
- OC: raw `Value` (unprocessed JSON-RPC result object).

**Edge cases**:
- CC: `isError:true` in result → `McpToolCallError` (not a transport error; server responded with a tool-level error).
- OC: `isError` field in result is not inspected; caller receives the error payload as success `Value`.
- CC: Binary/image blobs are resized and delivered as `image` content blocks.
- OC: No binary handling; `read_resource` (not `call_tool`) handles blob/text distinction.

**Error path**:
- CC: 401 → `McpAuthError`; -32001/session → `McpSessionExpiredError` + cache clear + retry; AbortError → silent `undefined`.
- OC: All errors surface as `McpError` variants: `Transport`, `Protocol`, `ToolNotFound`, `Timeout`. No auth-error or session-expiry distinctions.

**Discovered gap**: OC does not inspect `isError` in tool call results. Filed as gap.

---

### B4 — Resource capability gate: `list_resources` / `fetchResourcesForClient`

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:2000-2031` — `fetchResourcesForClient` (LRU-memoized). Guards on `client.capabilities?.resources` truthy. If absent, returns `[]` immediately — no wire call. Issues `{method:'resources/list'}` validated against `ListResourcesResultSchema`. Attaches `server: client.name` to each resource. Failure logs and returns `[]`.

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:515-542` — `McpServer::list_resources`. Guards on `self.has_resources()` (line 516), which checks `self.capabilities.resources.is_some()` (line 507). Returns `Ok(Vec::new())` immediately if absent — correct behavior, matching CC. Issues `resources/list` with `Some(json!({}))` as params (CC sends no params object).

**Input**: None (CC: keyed by client reference).

**Output**: `Vec<McpResource>` (OC) / `ServerResource[]` with `.server` field (CC).

**Edge cases**:
- CC: `result.resources` undefined → returns `[]` (no panic).
- OC: `result.get("resources").and_then(…).unwrap_or_default()` → same safe fallback.
- CC: Capability check is on `capabilities.resources` truthy (any object). OC: `capabilities.resources.is_some()` — equivalent.
- OC sends `Some(json!({}))` as params; CC sends no params. MCP spec treats params as optional; difference is benign but a divergence.

**Error path**:
- CC: Returns `[]` on error (logged). Never propagates.
- OC: Propagates `McpError` to caller; single-server `list_resources` failure aborts; multi-server path (in `McpManager::list_resources` line 757-768) catches per-server errors and logs, continuing for other servers.

**Assessment**: This behavior is substantially correct in OC. Minor param divergence noted.

---

### B5 — Unknown / non-standard server error code surfacing

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:1955-1966` — in `callMCPTool` catch block: if `error.constructor.name === 'McpError'` and `'code' in error` and `typeof error.code === 'number'`, wraps as `TelemetrySafeError` with message `McpError <code>`. The numeric code (e.g., -32000 ConnectionClosed, -32001 RequestTimeout, arbitrary server codes) is preserved in the error message. Upstream: SDK throws `McpError` with the raw code from the JSON-RPC error object.

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:249-263` — JSON-RPC error path in `StdioTransport::request`: `McpError::Protocol(format!("RPC error {}: {}{}", error.code, error.message, data_info))`. The numeric code is embedded in the protocol error string. `HttpTransport::request` at line 330: `McpError::Protocol(format!("RPC error {}: {}", error.code, error.message))` — data field dropped.

**Input**: JSON-RPC response with `error: {code: <any i64>, message: string, data?: Value}`.

**Output**: An error is returned — not panicked, not silently swallowed. The code is surfaced in the error message.

**Edge cases**:
- Unknown code (not -32600, -32700, etc.): still formatted as `RPC error <n>: <message>`. No special mapping needed.
- CC maps -32000 to "session expired" logic in specific transport contexts (HTTP/claudeai-proxy).
- OC: code -32000 is treated identically to any other code — no special session-expiry handling.

**Error path**:
- OC: `McpError::Protocol` is returned via `?` propagation. No panic path.
- CC: Caught in `callMCPTool` catch, re-thrown as telemetry-safe wrapper with code in message.

**Assessment**: OC correctly surfaces unknown codes without panicking. Missing: HTTP transport drops the `data` field from JSON-RPC errors (line 330 vs line 255-259 of stdio path which includes it). Filed as gap.

---

### B6 — Transport disconnect mid-call returns explicit error

**CC**: `services/mcp/client.ts:1234-1402` — connection-drop detection. `client.onerror` handler intercepts terminal errors: `ECONNRESET`, `ETIMEDOUT`, `EPIPE`, `EHOSTUNREACH`, `ECONNREFUSED`, `Body Timeout Error`, `terminated`, `SSE stream disconnected`, `Failed to reconnect SSE stream`. After `MAX_ERRORS_BEFORE_RECONNECT` (3) consecutive terminal errors, calls `closeTransportAndRejectPending()` which calls `client.close()`. SDK's `close()` rejects all pending `callTool` promises with `McpError -32000 "Connection closed"`. `onclose` clears the memoize cache so next call reconnects. SSE "Maximum reconnection attempts" also triggers `closeTransportAndRejectPending`. HTTP 404/-32001 triggers session-expiry path (`McpSessionExpiredError`, cache clear, retry).

**OC**: `src/mcp.rs:195-213` — `StdioTransport::request`: stdin write failures (`write_all`, flush) return `McpError::Transport`. Stdout read failure returns `McpError::Transport`. `HttpTransport::request` at line 313-319: `reqwest` send failure → `McpError::Transport`; HTTP non-2xx → `McpError::Transport`. In both: pending request is in a sequential lock (`reader: Mutex<BufReader<…>>`), so concurrent calls are serialized — only one in-flight at a time. No reconnection logic; no `onclose`/`onerror` hooks; no error-count-based escalation.

**Input**: In-flight `transport.request(…)` call when the transport layer fails.

**Output**: `McpError::Transport(String)` — explicit error, no panic.

**Edge cases**:
- OC: `StdioTransport` holds a single `Mutex<BufReader>`, so concurrent callers queue; a disconnect mid-read returns an IO error and subsequent callers will also error on the poisoned reader.
- CC: SDK handles concurrent requests with separate response routing; a disconnect rejects all pending promises simultaneously.
- OC: No reconnection attempt after transport error; caller must create a new `McpManager::connect_stdio/http`.
- CC: `onclose` clears the memoize cache; next `connectToServer` call transparently reconnects.

**Error path**:
- OC: `McpError::Transport` propagated up from the transport, through `call_tool`, to the `McpManager` caller.
- CC: `McpError -32000 "Connection closed"` surfaces from pending `callTool` futures when the SDK transport is closed.

---

## Additional discovered gaps

The four gaps from the issue brief plus newly discovered ones:

### Gap A (from brief): MCP elicitation missing
CC (`services/mcp/elicitationHandler.ts:68-313`, `client.ts:1191-1197`, `client.ts:999`) registers an `ElicitRequestSchema` handler on the SDK client. OC does not declare `elicitation` in `initialize` capabilities (`src/mcp.rs:382-389`) and has no elicitation handler. Servers that send elicitation requests will receive no response from OC.

### Gap B (from brief): MCP enterprise IdP OAuth (XAA) missing
CC (`services/mcp/xaa.ts`, `services/mcp/xaaIdpLogin.ts`) implements RFC 8693 token exchange + RFC 7523 JWT bearer grant for headless enterprise SSO. OC has no OAuth token management of any kind.

### Gap C (from brief): Per-server channel permissions missing
CC (`services/mcp/channelPermissions.ts`) races permission approvals against channel servers (Telegram, iMessage, Discord) that emit `notifications/claude/channel/permission` events. OC has no permission relay subsystem.

### Gap D (from brief): InProcessTransport missing
CC (`services/mcp/InProcessTransport.ts:1-65`) provides `createLinkedTransportPair()` used for in-process MCP servers (Computer Use, Chrome MCP, SDK-mode servers). OC `McpTransport` trait has no in-process implementation.

### Gap E (discovered): `isError` flag not inspected in tool call results
**CC**: `client.ts:3124-3148` checks `result.isError === true` and throws `McpToolCallError`. **OC**: `src/mcp.rs:499-501` returns the raw `Value` without checking `isError`. Callers receive a payload that contains an error description as a success value.

### Gap F (discovered): HTTP transport drops `data` field from JSON-RPC errors
**CC**: SDK preserves the full `data` field in `McpError`. **OC**: `HttpTransport::request` at `src/mcp.rs:329-332` formats error as `"RPC error {code}: {message}"` — `data` is discarded. `StdioTransport::request` at `src/mcp.rs:249-259` does include it. Asymmetry means HTTP callers lose structured error context.

### Gap G (discovered): `refresh_tools` ignores `capabilities.tools` gate
**CC**: `client.ts:1748-1751` returns `[]` without a wire call if `!client.capabilities?.tools`. **OC**: `src/mcp.rs:439-440` calls `tools/list` unconditionally. This is a spurious RPC call and will produce a JSON-RPC error if the server does not support the tools capability.

### Gap H (discovered): No connection-establishment timeout
**CC**: `client.ts:1048-1077` races `client.connect(transport)` against `getConnectionTimeoutMs()` ms (env-configurable). **OC**: `McpServer::new` → `initialize` → `transport.request("initialize", …)` has no timeout (the `Duration` timeout is only on `McpManager::call_tool_with_timeout`, not on handshake). A non-responsive server hangs the process indefinitely.

### Gap I (discovered): No reconnection / cache-clear after disconnect
**CC**: `client.ts:1374-1401` clears the memoize cache in `onclose` so the next `connectToServer` call reconnects transparently. **OC**: `McpManager` holds `HashMap<String, McpServer>` with no `onclose` hook; a disconnected server stays in the map with a broken transport; future calls return `McpError::Transport` with no self-healing.

### Gap J (discovered): SSE, WebSocket, Streamable-HTTP transports missing
**CC**: `client.ts:619-960` supports 8 transport types: `stdio`, `sse`, `sse-ide`, `ws`, `ws-ide`, `http` (StreamableHTTP), `sdk`, `claudeai-proxy`. **OC**: `src/mcp.rs` implements only `StdioTransport` (line 139) and `HttpTransport` (line 276, plain JSON-RPC POST — not Streamable HTTP). SSE, WebSocket, and the full MCP Streamable HTTP spec are absent.

### Gap K (discovered): HTTP transport sends plain JSON-RPC POST, not Streamable-HTTP
**CC**: `client.ts:784-865` uses `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` (SSE-capable bidirectional streaming, `application/json, text/event-stream` Accept header). **OC**: `HttpTransport::request` at `src/mcp.rs:297-337` sends a plain `POST` with `Content-Type: application/json` and deserializes a single JSON response — incompatible with MCP's Streamable HTTP spec which multiplexes server-sent events over the same connection. This makes the HTTP transport non-compliant with MCP 2025-03-26.

## Done when

- This issue body has been edited to contain a markdown spec, one section per behavior above
- Each behavior section names the function(s) on both sides (or marks the OC side missing)
- Every gap above has been filed as its own issue and linked here
- The matching Phase 2 issue is unblocked

Blocked by: (none)
Blocking: #543
